claim: setActive runs two UPDATE statements without a transaction. If the app crashes or the second UPDATE fails after the first one deactivates all vehicles, no vehicle is marked active, leaving the app in a broken state.
evidence: src/db/queries/vehicles.ts:152 first UPDATE deactivates all: 'UPDATE vehicle SET isActive = 0 WHERE isActive = 1'; src/db/queries/vehicles.ts:156 second UPDATE activates one: 'UPDATE vehicle SET isActive = 1 WHERE id = ?'. No db.withTransactionAsync wrapper.
runtimeTrigger: User switches active vehicle while a concurrent DB operation holds a write lock, or the process is killed between the two UPDATE calls.

claim: setForEvent DELETEs all service type links then re-INSERTs them individually without a transaction. If any INSERT fails mid-way, the event permanently loses some or all of its service type associations.
evidence: src/db/queries/eventServiceTypes.ts:55 DELETE: 'DELETE FROM event_service_type WHERE eventId = ?'; then line 59-62 loops INSERT one by one without transaction.
runtimeTrigger: User edits a service event and changes its service types while DB disk is full or process is interrupted during the loop.

claim: Deferred delete creates a 5-second window where the event is removed from Zustand state but still exists in SQLite. If loadForVehicle is called during this window (e.g., vehicle switch or pull-to-refresh), the deleted event reappears in the UI.
evidence: src/stores/eventStore.ts:228-231 removes from state immediately; line 233 sets a 5000ms setTimeout for actual DB delete. src/stores/eventStore.ts:81-93 loadForVehicle replaces state from DB query — will re-include the not-yet-deleted event.
runtimeTrigger: User deletes an event, then switches vehicle and switches back within 5 seconds. The deleted event reappears because loadForVehicle reads it from DB.

claim: When updating event photos, existing photos' sortOrder is never updated in the DB. Only newly added photos get a sortOrder from the loop counter. If the user reorders photos (moving existing photos to new positions), the existing photos retain their old sortOrder values, causing incorrect display order after re-opening the event.
evidence: src/stores/eventStore.ts:156-162: loop increments sortOrder for ALL URIs but only calls eventPhotoQueries.insert for new URIs. Existing photos' sortOrder is never updated via an UPDATE query.
runtimeTrigger: User edits an event, rearranges the order of existing photos, and saves. Re-opening the event shows photos in the original (database) order rather than the user's reordered positions.

claim: toISODate uses date.toISOString().split('T')[0] which converts to UTC, potentially shifting the selected date by -1 day for users in high positive UTC offsets (UTC+12, UTC+13). The DateTimePicker returns a Date set to noon local time; for UTC+13, noon local is 23:00 UTC of the previous day.
evidence: src/components/DateField.tsx:27: return date.toISOString().split('T')[0]; — toISOString always outputs UTC. Line 40: dateValue initialized with 'T12:00:00' (noon local). For UTC+13: noon local Jan 15 = 2024-01-14T23:00:00.000Z → toISODate returns '2024-01-14' instead of '2024-01-15'.
runtimeTrigger: User in Samoa (UTC+13) or Tonga picks a date in the DateTimePicker. The saved date is one day earlier than what they selected.

claim: updateSetting has no try/catch and no error state. If the DB write fails, the rejected promise propagates unhandled to the UI (called from Pressable onPress callbacks without try/catch), causing an unhandled promise rejection. This violates the project standard that errors must never be swallowed and every catch block surfaces a human-readable message.
evidence: src/stores/settingsStore.ts:36-42: async updateSetting has no try/catch. All other stores (vehicleStore, eventStore, reminderStore) wrap DB calls in try/catch and set an error state. Callers in settings.tsx:330 (onSelect: (v) => updateSetting('currency', v)) also have no error handling.
runtimeTrigger: User changes currency or theme while the database is temporarily locked by a concurrent operation. The settings change silently fails with an unhandled rejection.
